citation: Lanter, J., Norton, S., Wright, A., Porter, M., & Costello, D. (2023). Round Table: Addressing Censorship. *GameRT/GNCRT Publication*.
summary:
# Summary

The provided text is a professional resource document titled "Round Table: Addressing Censorship," authored by Julia Lanter, Shawn Norton, Amie Wright, Molly Porter, and Danielle Costello in 2023. It represents a collaborative effort between the Graphic Novels & Comics Round Table (GNCRT) Addressing Challenges Committee and the Games and Gaming Roundtable (GameRT). The document does not present original empirical research but rather serves as a comprehensive bibliography and advisory guide for library professionals dealing with censorship issues related to comics, graphic novels, video games, and role-playing games.

The primary objective of the text is to equip librarians with practical tools and historical context to address challenges to their collections. The authors curate a wide array of resources categorized into toolkits, articles, webinars, podcasts, libguides, books, websites, and videos. Key resources highlighted include the "GNCRT Addressing Challenges TOOLKIT" and the "Urban Libraries Unite (ULU) Drag Story Hour Support workbook." The document also references specific articles that provide historical perspective on media panics, such as Olivia Kennedy’s piece on Dungeons & Dragons during the 1980s Satanic Panic and Kelly Jensen’s analysis of books targeted by Moms for Liberty. These references underscore the recurring nature of censorship efforts against gaming and comic materials throughout history.

Regarding methodology, the text functions as a curated list rather than a study. It aggregates existing professional development materials, such as ALA Office for Intellectual Freedom webinars and GNCRT webinar series on handling challenges before, during, and after they occur. The authors also include recommendations for further reading, citing books like Joseph Laycock’s *Dangerous Games* and David Hadju’s *The Ten-Cent Plague*, which explore the sociological and historical impacts of moral panics over play and comics.

The document outlines several strategic implications for library practice. In the "Additional Notes" section, the authors emphasize that a robust Collection Development Plan is essential. They assert that community outreach is key and that public support serves as an important resource in defending collections. Furthermore, they recommend working on legislative alternatives and lobbying state and local officials. The text also includes a "GameRT Twitch Policy," outlining rules for respectful interaction and moderation on their streaming platform, indicating the relevance of digital space management in modern library gaming services.

Finally, the document promotes upcoming programming, including a Spring SUPER! Spectacular celebrating National Superhero Day, featuring activities like dramatic comics readings and liveplay sessions. This highlights the role of active programming in fostering community engagement and demonstrating the value of these materials to patrons. Overall, the text serves as a vital reference point for librarians seeking to navigate the complex landscape of censorship affecting games and graphic media, providing both defensive tools and historical context.

Round Table Round Table:
Addressing Censorship
with Julia Lanter, GNCRT Addressing Challenges Committee Shawn Norton, GNCRT Addressing Challenges Committee Amie Wright, GNCRT Addressing Challenges Committee Chair Molly Porter, GameRT Member, Programming Committee
Danielle Costello, GameRT Programing Co-Chair
Censorship Resources....................................................................................................................................... 1 Toolkits............................................................................................................................................................ 1 Articles.............................................................................................................................................................1 Webinars & Courses....................................................................................................................................... 2 Podcasts:.........................................................................................................................................................2 Libguides......................................................................................................................................................... 2 Books.............................................................................................................................................................. 2 Websites..........................................................................................................................................................2 Videos............................................................................................................................................................. 2
Additional Notes..................................................................................................................................................2 GameRT Twitch Policy........................................................................................................................................3 Learn More About GNCRT..................................................................................................................................3 Learn More About GameRT................................................................................................................................3
Censorship Resources
Toolkits
● GNCRT Addressing Challenges TOOLKIT (+ postcard version) ● Urban Libraries Unite (ULU) Drag Story Hour Support workbook
Articles
● “GNCRT seeks information about censorship of comics in schools and libraries” Tina Coleman, ALA Member News, April 15, 2022 ○ announced the launch of GNCRT’s survey of library workers’ experiences with comic book bans and challenges

● “How Dungeons & Dragons became a part of the 1980s Satanic Panic” Olivia Kennedy, DiceBreaker, June 9, 2022
● “Here are the Comics Moms for Liberty and Other Book Banners Call Inappropriate, and Why” Kelly Jensen, Book Riot, March 1, 2023
● “Libraries and Violent Media” Deborah Caldwell-Stone, Intellectual Freedom Blog, February 7, 2013 ● “Psychologists: linking video games to violence ‘not scientifically sound’” Charlie Hall, Polygon, Mar 4,
2020 ● “The Real History Behind Stranger Things 4’s Satanic Panic Plot” Jon Peterson, Polygon, May 27, 2022 ● “A Timeline of Video Game Controversies” - National Coalition Against Censorship, 2021 ● “Trade Secrets: Addressing Challenges to Comics in Libraries” Amie Wright, Booklist, February 1, 2022
○ announced the GNCRT Addressing Challenges Committee launch
Webinars & Courses
● ALA Office for Intellectual Freedom YouTube Channel ○ Frosty Windows, Frosty Mirrors: Representation, Labeling, Discoverability, and the Chilling Effect ALA Intellectual Freedom Round Table, February 21, 2021, youtube
● “Be Prepared! The GNCRT Guide to Addressing Comic Book Bans, Censorship, and Challenges” GNCRT & Booklist, July 21, 2022, webinar recording ○ registration required to access recording
● GNCRT Addressing Challenges webinar series ○ “Before a Challenge” - January 18, 2023 ○ “During a Challenge”- March 15, 2023
● Intellectual Freedom: Fighting the Good Fight Martin Garnar, Massachusetts Library System, vimeo ● Previous GNCRT webinars for Banned Books Week and Freedom to Read ● Talking to Press and Patrons Intellectual Freedom Committee, Connecticut Library Consortium,
youtube
Podcasts:
● “Comic Book Bans and Challenges” GNCRT & Australian Library and Information Association (ALIA) Graphic, September 18, 2022
Libguides
● American Comics Self-Censorship Comics Code (1954), New York Public Library
Books
● Dangerous Games What the Moral Panic over Role-Playing Games Says about Play, Religion, and Imagined Worlds, by Joseph Laycock
● Pinball: A Graphic History of the Silver Ball, by Jon Chad ● The Ten-Cent Plague: The Great Comic Book Scare and How It Changed America, by David Hadju

Websites
● Banned & Challenged Books ● Fight Censorship ● Penguin Random House Banned Books Resources Hub ● The Uncensored Library
○ Reporters Without Borders using a Minecraft server to subvert censorship across nations ● Unite Against Book Bans ● EveryLibrary
Videos
● Banned Books: When Books Are Threatened, Where Do We Turn? Random House, youtube ● Dungeons & Dragons: Adventure or Abomination RPG Gamer, youtube ● Dungeons & Dragons: Satanic Panic | Retro Report New York Times, youtube ● Early Censorship in Video Games Jenovi, youtube ● Variant Cover with Chris Vasquez, Washington Post, tiktok
Additional Notes
● COLLECTION DEVELOPMENT PLAN!!! ● Community outreach is key! Public support is an important resource ● Work on legislative alternatives, lobbying state and local officials ● Please check out our GNCRT Addressing Challenges TOOLKIT (+ postcard version) for more advice
on collection development plans and challenge policies
GameRT Twitch Policy
1. Be kind and respectful to one another 2. Hate speech of any type will not be tolerated 3. We reserve the right to remove anyone from the chat for any reason 4. If you are feeling harassed by anyone, please notify a GameRT twitch mod and one of us will assist you 5. If you'd rather talk privately about anything, please send us a direct message
Learn More About GNCRT
Facebook: ALA Graphic Novels & Comics Round Table Twitter: @libcomix Instagram: @libcomix Discord: GNCRT Website: GNCRT ALA Connect page
Learn More About GameRT
Blog: bit.ly/GameRTBlog Facebook: bit.ly/GameRTLeague & bit.ly/GameRTFacebook

Resources: bit.ly/GameRTPublicResources Website: bit.ly/GameRTWebsite Questions? Email gamert@ala.org

Upcoming Programs

ALA's Games and Gaming Roundtable (GameRT), the Comics and Graphic Novels Roundtable (GNCRT), and

the Film and Media Roundtable (FMRT) will host a Spring SUPER! Spectacular on Friday, April 28, 2023,

celebrating National Superhero Day on GameRT’s Twitch channel.

● 11:00 - 11:45 CST ● 12:00 - 12:45 CST ● 1:00 - 1:30 CST ● 1:45 - 2:45 CST
Scripts ● 3:00 - 4:00 CST

Cape & Bureau Liveplay
